title: ceratostylis arfakensis
intro: Ceratostylis arfakensis, also known as the Arfak orchid, is a species of orchid native to the Arfak Mountains in West Papua, Indonesia. This epiphytic orchid has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Ceratostylis arfakensis typically involve infusing the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, which is then used to treat various ailments.
